name: Savory Greek Cheese Pies
method: In a measuring bowl, weigh ¼ cup of hot water.
Mix in the water with the yeast and sugars and then let sit until it rises
about five minutes.
Sift the flour with the salts in a paddle-equipped stand mixer.
Load the Greek yogurt and the vegetable oil into the liquid
combination.
Mix until a softball shapes the paste, and the dough is flexible and
convenient to treat. If the dough tends to be dry, add some more hot
water.
Add a little flour if it is too heavy. Cover and enable the dough to rest
for around an hour just until the amount has doubled.
Smash the dough downwards to deflate after that last resting period
and roll the dough over to a finely floured surface.
Split it into twelve equal parts, roughly the equivalent of a normal
walnut.
With a knife, break the feta cheese and position it in a moderate dish.
Within the cup, crack the egg and mix with a spoon until you
have a stable combination.
Heat the oven to 375°F.
Work with one slice of flour at the moment and push it with
your palms to make a 3-inch disk.
Fill every round with around a tablespoon of the coating,
make sure that it is not stretched to the dough's sides or you can not
properly cover it.
Stretch the dough over the filler and push down to close with a
spoon.
Place it on a cookie sheet lined with parchment paper.
Position the sheet pan on the middle shelf of the preheated
oven for 20 to 30 minutes or until golden brown.
Spray the cheese pies gently with the egg mixture, and scatter
on any sesame seeds.
Pull them out from the oven and position them to chill on a
serving plate.
Eat them at ambient temperature or hotter.
Cover thinly with plastic wrap to store and keep for one or
several hours at room temperature, but they probably won't survive
that long.
